project.title: Characterisation of HMGB1 interactome in PNT2 prostate cells
project.projectDescription: HMGB1 and HMGB2 are members of the High Mobility Group (HMG) protein superfamily that contain a DNA binding domain (HMG-Box), and their overexpression has been related to main cancer hallmarks, tumor progression, metastasis formation and bad prognosis. We have used yeast two hybrid and affinity purification-mass spectrometry to explore HMGB1 and HMGB2 protein interactions in prostate and ovarian epithelial cells.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Protein extracts from PNT2 cells were immunoprecipitated with an HMGB1 antibody coupled to Protein G Dynabeads. Immunoprecipitated proteins were digested on the beads overnight with trypsin. Peptides were dried, redissolved in 20 mM TCEP-0.5% formic acid and analysed on an Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer coupled with an Ultimate 3000 RSLCnano System. The Orbitrap Velos was operated in standard data-dependent acquisition.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The raw files were processed with Proteome Discoverer v1.4 (Thermo Fisher Scientific). Database searches were performed with Mascot (Matrix Science) against the human Uniprot database (2014, 77606 entries) and an in-house contaminant database. The search parameters were: trypsin/P digestion, 2 missed cleavages, 10 ppm mass tolerance for MS, 0.5 Da mass tolerance for MS/MS, with variable modifications of acetyl (N-terminal), carbamidomethyl (C), N-formylation (protein), oxidation (M), and pyro-glu (N-term Q). Database search results were refined through processing with Percolator (significance threshold < 0.05, FDR < 1%). Protein identification required at least one high-confidence peptide (FDR < 1%) with a minimum score of 20.

references.referenceLine: Barreiro-Alonso A, Lamas-Maceiras M, García-Díaz R, Rodríguez-Belmonte E, Yu L, Pardo M, Choudhary JS, Cerdán ME. Delineating the HMGB1 and HMGB2 interactome in prostate and ovary epithelial cells and its relationship with cancer. Oncotarget. 2018 9(27):19050-19064

Characteristics[CellLine]: Immortalized human benign prostate epithelial cells PNT2
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Velos mass spectrometer coupled with an Ultimate 3000 RSLCnano System.
Comment[GradientTime]: a 120 min linear gradient of 4–32% CH3CN/0.1% formic acid at 300 nL/min, and the total cycle time was 150 min (from AB07)
Comment[Separation]: analytical column (75 µm id × 25 cm) (PepMap C18, 2 µ)
